author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text:
Thanks for everything, Rob, you certainly deserve everything you've worked for. And thank you very much for sharing.
Might I humbly suggest you do just ONE example video, that is in four parts: Maybe with a white-board presentation showing the channel structure.
     Part 1, The basic (freebie) content.
     Part 2, The £1.99 content (highlighting the 'extra')
     Part 3, The £4.99    "    "    "
     Part 4, The £8.99     "    "    ".
I only ask this as I'm sure I'm not alone in being unable to visualise the structure/benefits out of just hearing the 'jargon' of "discord", "VIP" etc. It would certainly help me to decide what and how to become a member of the appropriate level. Thanks.

author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text: Member changes? I guess it happens to the best of us. But I was blaming it on agricultural run-off, why my rhubarb was wilting.  :-/

replies.text: cheers mate

author: @rayadagio
text: I can fully understand the desire to maximize profits with one's full-time project. However, I can also imagine that there is a certain undertone for all the viewers who have contributed to the channel's growth with their subscriptions, engaged comments, and likes, undoubtedly generating significant advertising revenue, only to now be relegated to fourth-class viewers.

author: @ginner43
text:
Seems a fair and sensible structure. I have learnt a lot from your channels and knowledge is worth more than money. It costs to make each video and you need to raise the funds one way or another. I sometimes have big gaps between my videos simply because of the cost of the materials needed.
Good work and long may you continue.

author: @secretrat
text:
There are just too many channels doing this. I subscribe to hundreds of channels and even at $1 each, it is unaffordable. 

I thoroughly enjoy your videos and I think your content is first rate. I am content to put up with a little advertising as I know it pays the bills.  

I will continue to follow ad supported content, but that is as far as I'll go. I don't expect this to be the case, but I imagine if the value of your ad supported content decreases, I'll be less interested and will be watching some other channel. That would indeed be a pity.

text: Listen, brother,  I am happy to do this, and halfway through your video went vip. However, as you yourself know, it's expensive to do a lot of these recreations. I was extremely sad when I let me membership laps and lost my stamp.. lol seems silly, but I am a proud follower.

author: @thomashverring
text: Understandable. This happens eventually to channels. I liked how democratic this was. When you have to pay to get the full experience most won't even think about it but those who are poor are left outside of the conversation. I've experienced this so many times now. It's sad.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Sorry mate but the channel costs to run and i takes a lot to do it - i do it on a shoestring but the workload is terrible

replies.author: @thomashverring
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I know. I understand. The world is sadly not for everyone. After becoming sick and poor I experience this a lot. Many things I took for granted before, things I did, tools I used, they are just beyond my reach now. I can still watch your free stuff, maybe some day I can afford the basic tier, but the VIPs are the only true members from now on.

replies.author: @bikerfirefarter7280
replies.text: @thomashverring Becoming sick AND poor. Thats just unfair. I hope you find a way to address at least one of them. Cultivating the right attitude is important. Keep your chin up. Work with what you've got. There are plenty of examples of people overcoming various obstacles. Look to them to build your mind-set. Peace, from UK.

author: @marcfruchtman9473
text: Hello Robert -- I recently spotted an article about something called a "Heat battery". It basically uses water vapour and a salt K2CO3 (potassium carbonate) in a Storage cycle: moistening (which yields the heat of dissolution) and drying the salt with a thermochemical heat exchange to the environment. It was coming out of research from Eindhoven University of Technology.(Might be worth looking into)?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice on mate - thanks for the heads up

author: @peterbrown172
text: Sad to see the channel degenerate to this. What is cheap for the British, Europeans and Americans is really costly for many people in less fortunate countries. I wish you well and hope to still be able to nibble on the scraps you might condescend to toss to the rabble. If it were REALLY about the likes, there wouldn’t be a need for elitism. A sad day.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: can't you see the cost of running a channel?

replies.author: @FasutonemuMyoji
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering One Idea I've seen before is running a live stream an hour or two every month and the moderators in the chat can give away random free memberships. You don't really even need to be there if its a premier video. This way some random amount of people who are super fans but can't afford it still get a free month, and it generates buzz, a win win.

author: @crazymonkeyVII
text: Your timing with this one could not be more perfect :). I'm not really using Discord much so I'm no expert, but when I click on that link to discord all I get is "You find yourself in a strange place, you don't have access to any text channels, or there are none in this server". Is that supposed to happen? Anyway, I just wanted to thank you for your great efforts. I found your videos a month ago, and I thank you for all your hard work making these inspiring and interesting videos. I've spend hours in fascination watching your content over the last few weeks, and I'm astonished at the rate at which you manage to present interesting, inspiring videos! I definitely think that hard work should be supported. Thank you so much sir, please take my money 😂.
